Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And For Order Setting Aside Default
Off Calendar Aug-23-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And For Order Setting Aside Default.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Yan's motion to quash/vacate default is granted.

Yan declares that Bryant Fu never served him with a copy of the summons and complaint. That declaration curiously fails to explain how Yan learned of this case or how "by chance" he discovered the purportedly false proof of service days after it was filed with the court on March 21, 2023.

In opposition, plaintiff Fu cites the proof of service which shows that Yan was allegedly served on September 26, 2022, at the court. It is unclear why this proof of service was filed nearly six months later. Moreover, Bryant Fu does not provide a declaration amplifying the precise circumstances surrounding the alleged service.

Based on this vague and disputed record, the court grants the motion to quash/vacate. "Because the law favors disposing of cases on their merits, 'any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default [citations].'" (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.).

Counsel or the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. CRC 3.1312 compliance is required.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Bailiff: Nguyen. (301/RCE)
